title: Admin
overview_intro: Inspect the last 7 days, all-time totals, and recent activity across the system.
overview_empty_desc: Try again. If audit logs are empty, the daily summary and recent activity will stay at 0.
overview_daily_trend_empty_desc: The chart will appear here once audit activity is recorded across the last 7 days.
overview_summary_desc: Current cumulative totals across the whole system, independent of the 7-day trend window.
overview_daily_reports_desc: Key actions for the last {{days}} days, grouped by UTC calendar day.
overview_recent_events_desc: Latest audit events in reverse chronological order.
overview_recent_events_empty_desc: Recent audit events will appear here once activity is recorded.
users_intro: Manage user accounts, roles, and storage quotas.
policies_intro: Configure storage policies, upload modes, and default file placement.
shares_intro: Review and manage active share links across the system.
locks_intro: Inspect active WebDAV locks and clear them when necessary.
settings_intro: Manage system config entries and custom runtime settings.
audit_intro: Review important activity records and filter audit events by criteria.
confirm_delete_policy: Are you sure you want to delete this policy?
confirm_force_delete: This will permanently delete the user and all their data.
confirm_purge: This will permanently delete the item. This cannot be undone.
confirm_empty_trash: This will permanently delete all items in trash.
no_shares_desc: Active share links will appear here.
no_policies_desc: Storage policies control where files are stored.
no_policies_assigned: No policies assigned. Using system default.
force_unlock_desc: This will release the WebDAV lock. Clients holding this lock will encounter errors.
delete_share_desc: The share link will stop working immediately.
delete_policy_desc: This will not delete stored files, but new uploads using this policy will fail.
delete_config_desc: This config entry will be permanently removed.
s3_endpoint_hint: For Cloudflare R2, use the account-level S3 endpoint root such as https://<account>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com. Do not use a public .r2.dev URL; keep the bucket in the Bucket field. If /<bucket> is entered by mistake, the UI will split it into the Bucket field when possible, and the backend still validates it as a fallback.
s3_endpoint_public_r2_dev_error: Detected a public r2.dev URL. This field must use the account-level Cloudflare R2 S3 API endpoint, not a public bucket domain.
s3_upload_strategy_proxy_tempfile_desc: The browser uploads to AsterDrive first, then the server writes a local temporary file before sending it to S3. Most compatible, but it uses local temporary disk space.
s3_upload_strategy_relay_stream_desc: The browser uploads to AsterDrive, and the server relays the bytes directly to S3 without writing local temporary files. relay_stream does not perform SHA256 deduplication.
s3_upload_strategy_presigned_desc: Upload directly to S3 via presigned URLs. Files ≤ chunk size use single PUT; larger files use S3 multipart upload with resume support. Requires bucket CORS with ExposeHeaders: ["ETag"].
chunk_size_desc: Files larger than this are split into chunks. For S3 relay_stream and presigned modes, this also controls the multipart part size; S3 multipart still obeys the 5 MiB minimum part size. 0 = always use single upload.
policy_test_failed_confirm_desc: The current S3 configuration failed the connection test. You can still save it, but uploads using this policy may fail. Do you want to continue?
create_user_desc: Create a new account with the default quota and storage policy.
user_search_placeholder: Search by username, email, or ID...
no_filtered_users_desc: Try another keyword or adjust the filters.
storage_quota_settings_desc: Adjust the user's total quota here and manage policy assignments directly.
storage_policy_assignments_desc: Assign available policies to the user and choose the default storage target.
user_details_footer_hint: Changes are saved to the system immediately. Proceed carefully.
initial_admin_delete_blocked: The initial admin account cannot be deleted.
initial_admin_status_blocked: The initial admin account cannot be disabled.
initial_admin_role_blocked: The initial admin account cannot be demoted.
default_policy_required: A default policy must always exist while assignments remain.
default_policy_remove_blocked: Set another policy as default before removing the current default policy.
